title: KDNA Internal Module Manifest (IMM)
description: Declares, within a single domain package, which parts are independently loadable (sub_domain), which are internal tools (internal_module), and which are pure data (reference). Authoring-time only; never enters runtime .kdna container. See RFC-0013 §3.3.

domain_id.description: The KDNA domain this manifest belongs to.

modules.description: All modules in this domain. At least one module required.

module_id.description: Unique identifier for this module within the domain.

module_type.description: Per RFC-0013 §3.3: internal_module = in-domain, not independently loadable; sub_domain = independently loadable as its own .kdna; reference = pure data, not judgment.

independent_asset.description: Whether this module is independently loadable and publishable. MUST be true iff module_type is sub_domain; MUST be false if module_type is internal_module or reference.

maps_to.description: Where in KDNA_Core.json / KDNA_Patterns.json this module is realized, e.g., KDNA_Core.json.frameworks[core_principles]. Optional for reference modules.

loadable_via.description: Which load profile (per specs/load-profiles.md) can load this module. full = all six files. Required for internal_module and sub_domain. Forbidden for reference (reference modules are never loaded as judgment).

decomposition_rationale.description: Optional human-readable explanation of why a domain was kept monolithic rather than split. Read by PR-2 Anti-Monolithic lint as the maintainer sign-off for the Anti-Monolithic Domain principle.
